metadata.dataset_name: Cortex Air Mode - Block H Air Cargo Security and Screening Matrix
metadata.purpose: Mock/reference security status matrix and jurisdiction security rules for Cortex air-mode readiness checks.
metadata.legal_note: R&D/mock reference only. Aviation security rules are jurisdiction-, carrier-, station-, route-, commodity-, shipper-, and threat-level dependent. Final operational validation must come from carrier/station/security authority.
metadata.integration_note: Run Block H after Blocks B, C, D, F and before final readiness. Passenger aircraft routing requires passenger-eligible security status; EU-bound third-country cargo requires ACC3/secure supply-chain validation; PLACI lanes require complete advance cargo data.
recommended_next_research: Carrier-specific security acceptance rules by station
Known consignor / regulated agent datasets if available
Local screening facility capability for TUN and North Africa

security_status_matrix.description: Secured freight for passenger and all-cargo aircraft
security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Cargo may be eligible for passenger aircraft from a security standpoint if other blocks pass.
security_status_matrix.notes: Do not confuse security eligibility with DG/legal/carrier eligibility.

security_status_matrix.description: Cargo secure for all-cargo aircraft only
security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Security status allows all-cargo aircraft only; do not route on passenger belly unless status changes to passenger-eligible.
security_status_matrix.notes: Important for deciding passenger belly vs freighter/security routing.

security_status_matrix.description: Cargo not secured yet for passenger or all-cargo aircraft
security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Cargo is not security-ready. It requires screening/security process before loading.
security_status_matrix.notes: Hard gate until security status is resolved.

security_status_matrix.description: Security cargo - personal effects requiring prescreening
security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Personal effects or similar cargo need prescreening/security handling before uplift.

security_status_matrix.description: Cargo secure for passenger and all-cargo aircraft according to high-risk requirements
security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: High-risk cargo that has been secured under enhanced requirements. Still needs route/station validation.
security_status_matrix.notes: Never treat SHR as ordinary cargo. Keep security confidence capped without proof.

security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Theft-sensitive cargo requiring secure storage, chain of custody, controlled handover and often escort.
security_status_matrix.notes: Security status and secure storage are both needed; Block C/D/G interaction.

security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Cargo has theft, tamper, damage or vulnerability risk and needs controlled handling.
security_status_matrix.notes: Treat like security-sensitive even if not high-value.

security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Reserved/controlled booking cargo; security and operational coordination should be confirmed.
security_status_matrix.notes: Use mostly as booking/security coordination warning.

security_status_matrix.meaning_for_cortex: Fast transfer cargo; security status must be clear before transfer because there is little time to resolve screening gaps.
security_status_matrix.notes: If security status unknown, QRT is risky.

jurisdiction_security_rules.condition: cargo transported on passenger aircraft departing US airport or under TSA cargo program scope
jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: 100 percent cargo screening at level comparable to passenger checked baggage screening

jurisdiction_security_rules.condition: air carrier flies cargo/mail into EU from non-EU airport
jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: ACC3 designation for each non-EU airport; cargo/mail must be screened to EU standards or originate from EU-validated secure supply chain

jurisdiction_security_rules.condition: cargo is offered to EU-bound air carrier via known consignor/account consignor/regulated agent chain
jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: validate secure supply chain actor status or apply screening before carrier acceptance
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: European Commission air cargo and mail security

jurisdiction_security_rules.condition: destination or transit jurisdiction has PLACI/advance cargo information requirements
jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: submit/validate pre-loading advance cargo information before loading; ensure shipment data completeness
maintain Consignment Security Declaration / equivalent audit trail showing how, when and by whom cargo was secured
screen or secure cargo before treating it as ready for uplift
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from special handling code logic + aviation security rules

jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: route as all-cargo aircraft only unless security status is upgraded/validated for passenger aircraft
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from special handling code logic

jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: cargo can be treated as passenger/all-cargo security eligible only with station/carrier confirmation and other block checks
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from special handling code logic

jurisdiction_security_rules.condition: security_code = SCY or cargo is personal effects requiring prescreening
jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: prescreen before routing as ready cargo
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from special handling code logic

jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: confirm enhanced/high-risk screening and chain-of-custody evidence
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from IATA cargo security concepts

jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: secure storage, controlled handover, chain of custody, and station/carrier special-cargo confirmation
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from Block B/D/G security-sensitive cargo logic

jurisdiction_security_rules.required_security_action: security status must be resolved before transfer; unresolved NSC/SCY status blocks quick transfer readiness
jurisdiction_security_rules.source_name: Authored from special handling code logic

placi_minimum_data.notes: Needed for risk assessment and shipment identification.
Needed for risk assessment and shipment identification.
Vague descriptions should trigger confidence cap and data correction.
Used to validate shipment data consistency.
Used to validate shipment data consistency.
Needed to evaluate PLACI jurisdiction and risk.
Needed for shipment filing and traceability.
SPX/SCO/NSC/SCY/SHR or equivalent must be known.
CSD or equivalent security audit trail should be available when secured status is claimed.

confidence_rules.message: Cargo security status must be known before declaring shipment ready to fly.
Passenger aircraft cargo requires passenger-eligible screened/secured status.
Not secured cargo is not ready for uplift until screening/security process is completed.

confidence_rules.applies_to: EU inbound cargo from third-country airport
confidence_rules.message: EU-bound third-country cargo requires ACC3/secure supply-chain/screening validation.
Advance cargo information must be complete for PLACI-regulated lanes.
Secured cargo should have CSD/equivalent audit trail showing who secured it, when and how.
Valuable/vulnerable cargo needs secure storage and chain-of-custody confirmation.
Security matrix is advisory; station/carrier security confirmation is required for special cargo.

source_refs.use_in_dataset: CSD security audit trail concept: how, when and by whom cargo was secured.
100% cargo screening requirement for cargo transported on passenger aircraft under TSA cargo program.
ACC3 requirement for air carriers flying cargo/mail into EU from non-EU airports.

source_refs.source_name: European Commission air cargo and mail security
source_refs.use_in_dataset: EU secure supply chain actors and cargo/mail security framework.
PLACI jurisdictions and pre-loading advance cargo information rule family.
SPX, SCO, NSC, SCY, SHR, VAL, VUN, RAC, QRT security code mapping.
